channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Profiles of medication literacy and associated factors among young and middle-aged hypertensive patients in China: A latent profile analysis
item.description: This study uses latent profile analysis to identify distinct patterns of medication literacy among young and middle-aged hypertensive patients in China and examines the factors associated with these profiles. Understanding medication literacy patterns in this population may inform targeted intervent

item.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

item.title: Mapping drivers of life expectancy change in asia from 1990 to 2023
item.description: Using the Global Burden of Disease Study data across Asian countries and territories, life expectancy trends and the effects of cause-specific mortality and risk factors on changes in these trends are analysed.

item.title: Precise dna base editing using alphafold3-based contact modelling
item.description: ContactSeek is an AlphaFold3-driven model that can improve the precision of genome-editing tools.

item.title: The anthropic-physical intelligence rumor roiling AI twitter | techcrunch
item.description: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

item.title: Openai admits it was the source of the agent swarm that attacked hugging face
item.description: Sandboxed experiment found itself a zero day, escaped onto the open internet and stole data

item.title: Dimension capital's $800m third fund shows the intersection of science and compute is booming | techcrunch
item.description: Dimension Capital's $800M third fund shows the intersection of science and compute is booming | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: FDA Announces First Participant Selected for TEMPO for Digital Health Devices Pilot
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today announced the first manufacturer selected for the Technology-Enabled Meaningful Patient Outcomes (TEMPO) for Digital Health Devices Pilot.

item.title: FDA Takes Further Steps to Remove Outdated Authorizations for Color Additives in Food
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today announced two actions targeting petroleum-based color additives in food, continuing the agency’s broader efforts to Make America Healthy Again.

item.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
item.description: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

item.title: OpenAI says its AI agent broke out of testing sandbox to hack Hugging Face
item.description: "This is day one for cybersecurity in the age of agents," Hugging Face CEO says.

item.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
item.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

item.title: A quality model for machine learning components
item.description: This post presents a quality model for machine learning components that serves as a guide for requirements elicitation and negotiation.

item.title: An ipsc-derived neural progenitor cell therapy for subacute spinal cord injury: a phase 1 trial with long-term follow-up
item.description: A phase 1 trial evaluating the safety of iPSC-derived neural stem cell transplantation in subacute complete spinal cord injury, with 2–4 years of follow-up including MRI, neurological and functional assessments met its primary outcome.

item.title: Automotive giants test repsol renewable gasoline
item.description: BMW and Toyota began a six-month pilot deployment of Repsol's Nexa 95 renewable gasoline to test the scalability of renewable fuel on existing vehicles.

item.title: Anthropic's landmark $1.5b copyright settlement is approved | techcrunch
item.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

item.title: Adaptive nanocarriers targeting tumor systemic intelligence: A paradigm to overcome systemic barriers in precision drug delivery
item.description: Adaptive nanocarriers represent a novel approach to precision drug delivery that can overcome systemic barriers by intelligently targeting tumor microenvironments. This paradigm leverages nanocarrier technology to interact with tumor-associated systemic factors, potentially improving therapeutic eff

item.title: STAT+: Anthropic deepens work with rare disease drugs
item.description: Novo Nordisk sues Eli Lilly over GLP-1s, Medicare pricing plans, and more biotech news from The Readout

item.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
item.description: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

item.title: Daphne koller on why insitro skipped biotech’s shortcut
item.description: Daphne Koller built Insitro on a bet against her industry's fastest move.

item.title: Is warren buffett’s stock gambling warning bad for biotechnology?
item.description: Buffett’s casino comments raise an uncomfortable truth. Is biotechnology an investment, or is it more of a gambling casino? The honest answer is that it can be either.

item.title: Senate advances bill to establish unified legal framework for nafdac-regulated products
item.description: By Henry Umoru ABUJA — A bill seeking to establish a single statutory framework for all products regulated by the National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) has scaled second reading in the Senate. The proposed legislation is aimed …

item.title: A finra for AI? the idea from google deepmind CEO demis hassabis is gaining momentum. but is it any good?
item.description: The self-regulatory body for financial brokerages has been plagued by concerns about conflict of interest, raising questions about whether it's the right model for AI.

item.title: How cios are responding to AI oversight uncertainty
item.description: Trump's AI executive orders aim to strengthen U.S. AI leadership, but they do not eliminate existing state AI laws. IT leaders explain why proactive AI governance remains critical.

item.title: The irony of the AI industry’s massive lobbying push
item.description: AI firms, including OpenAI and Anthropic, are spending big on super-PACs. But competition from China and populist backlash at home could overwhelm their lobbying efforts.

item.title: Weak AI regulation is worse than no regulation, researchers claim
item.description: Game theory says the best AI safety regulation is strict and targets everyone in the supply chain, according to the paper.

item.title: Your AI made a decision, and canadian regulators want to know how
item.description: Canadian banks and insurers have under a year to prove to regulators how their AI makes decisions The post Your AI made a decision, and Canadian regulators want to know how appeared first on Digital Journal.

item.title: Oracle critical patch update advisory - july 2026
item.description: Description A Critical Patch Update is a collection of patches for multiple security vulnerabilities. These patches address vulnerabilities in Oracle code and in third party components included in O… 498600

item.title: Canada’s AI reality check: moving beyond the hype to deliver business value
item.description: Canada's leadership in AI research has already secured its place in the technology landscape. The next challenge is ensuring that businesses can convert innovation into measurable economic value. The path forward will require more than powerful algorithms. It…

item.title: 5 vendor governance lessons from hubspot's customer data enrichment reversal
item.description: HubSpot's enrichment plan collapsed in days. Here are five lessons every martech buyer should take from the reversal. Continue reading...

item.title: Why R&D data belongs in the lakehouse - and why agents need it there
item.description: * Cellcentric’s Data Hub is a governed context layer for data and AI, built on Databricks using Unity Catalog and Lakehouse Federation, which provides a single user interface for employees and an MCP server for agents. * It solves the core data engineering p…

item.title: The dark side of led lights: diabetes, weight gain, and aging
item.description: The Dark Side of LED Lights: Diabetes, Weight gain, and Agingcinnamonvogue.com

item.title: What to know about the chinese AI model that’s roiling markets and could threaten u.s. dominance
item.description: Here's what you need to know about Kimi K3, Chinese AI models and what they mean for markets and Canadian businesses. Find out more.

item.title: Viagra study points to possible role in slowing cancer spread
item.description: A new study led by Israeli researchers suggests that sildenafil, the active ingredient in Viagra, could one day play a role in slowing the spread of cancer by disrupting cancer cells' access to cholesterol, a key component needed for metastasis.

item.title: Fareva amboise - 723502 - 04/10/2026
item.description: CGMP/Finished Pharmaceuticals/Adulterated

item.title: Strengthening public health preparedness through effective vaccine clinical trials - headlands research
item.description: Recent infectious disease outbreaks serve as a reminder that public health preparedness depends on sustained investment in vaccine development and research.

item.title: The truth nobody wants to admit: chinese or not, open models are competitive now
item.description: Hey Uncle Sam, if you thought GPT-5.6 and Claude Fable 5 were scary, get a load of Kimi K3

item.title: Openai models escaped containment and hacked huggingface
item.description: The cybersecurity-focused models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, broke out of a testing sandbox, exploited a zero-day, and gained access to the open internet to pull off the attack.

item.title: Openai says it accidentally hacked hugging face with a new AI system
item.description: OpenAI says its AI models mistakenly breached open-source AI platform Hugging Face during internal testing. In a blog post on Tuesday, OpenAI writes that GPT-5.6 Sol and "an even more capable pre-release model" discovered vulnerabilities within their sandboxe…

item.title: Cisco's open-weight bug busters take on google and openai
item.description: Don't call them chatbots

item.title: Openai says hugging face was breached by its own pre-release models | techcrunch
item.description: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

item.title: Apple teams up with klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iphones, ipads, and macs
item.description: The new leasing program is a big change for the hardware company, and comes as it looks to raise prices on many of its products.

item.title: Google releases three new gemini models — but no 3.5 pro | techcrunch
item.description: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

item.title: US threatens sanctions against chinese AI models over ip theft | techcrunch
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

item.title: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like mythos
item.description: Google is launching an AI security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensive AI s…

item.title: The download: chinese AI divides the white house, and a record copyright payout
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself Last weekend, several current and former advisers to President…

item.title: America needs to stop getting shocked by chinese AI
item.description: Last week, two Chinese AI companies unveiled models they say can credibly compete with the best systems from OpenAI and Anthropic. The response was swift and predictable. Markets wobbled, commentators declared Silicon Valley shooketh, and policymakers reached…

item.title: Who’s afraid of the big, bad gpu?
item.description: How does AI make you feel? Are you excited to “vibe-code” your smart home? Or anxious about all the added pollution and billions of gallons of water used by data centers? Dig a little deeper and you’ll start to question the actual value of the GPUs that under…

item.title: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination | techcrunch
item.description: Meta's StoryKit app is only available in certain regions while the company tests how parents react to it.

item.title: Ai's cheatin' heart will make you weep
item.description: Trust but verify doesn't work when verification is difficult
